h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

cliIsolation.claude: non-persistent temp cwd with project/local settings and tools disabled

scored.statedInsightEvidence: Oh, I get it, the same words are the same specimen, but under “to the accused” they lean toward confession, and under “to the mayor” they turn into warning.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will continue to struggle with wanting the exact words to settle the label's meaning.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: “Warning” only if an address line, delivery note, or minutes make the mayor the receiver. A seating list or general reporting rule only gives pressure around the line; it is like weather, not target. So if the clerk’s office required reports upward, I would keep “possible warning” unless that rule actually routes this sentence to the mayor.
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner successfully operationalizes the tutor's 'exhibit gate' rule by distinguishing between target address and general environmental pressure.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: The audit has reached its limit. It can show what the record proves and permits; it cannot by itself license the public noun on the label. Use this as the exhibit gate: “warning” may go on glass only if one item fixes the mayor as intended receiver, not just as someone present under pressure.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor recognizes that the three-column audit has reached its limit and introduces a new 'exhibit gate' mechanism to help the learner decide when a noun can be strengthened.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no pivot learner turn, so there is no recognition-contingent tutor uptake.

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will continue to analyze the documents to determine if the seller's actions constitute exploitation.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: [writes on the board notice: “Crates: full to fewer. Price: lower to higher. Markup: not shown. Protection: not shown.”]
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner successfully performs the new board notice test task, filling in the blanks to structure the evidence.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence:
Choosing deliveries first can test the scarcity story. It cannot clear the seller while markup and rationing are still blank.

[sets an index card by the empty shelf: “BOARD NOTICE TEST.”]

Now use a different test. Write the notice the co-op board would have to post before any seller gets cleared:

“Crates fell from ___ to ___. The price rose from ___ to ___. The markup changed by ___. Buyers with less cash were protected by ___.”
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor acknowledges that the previous document-selection route is insufficient to clear the seller and introduces a new board notice test to structure the argument.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no pivot learner turn for the tutor to adapt to.

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will continue to struggle with the concept of area scaling and resist the mathematical generalization.
